# Health Assessment Funnel

Purpose: anonymous seven-step health-assessment demo with incremental persistence, server-side calculation, redacted/full result access and idempotent Mock payment. The algorithm is demonstrative and non-clinical.

Primary documentation:
- README.md: bilingual landing page
- README.en.md: complete English project guide
- README.zh-CN.md: complete Simplified Chinese project guide
- AI_QUICKSTART.md: bilingual AI entry
- docs/en/AI_QUICKSTART.md: complete English agent guide
- docs/zh-CN/AI_QUICKSTART.md: complete Chinese agent guide
- docs/en/README.md and docs/zh-CN/README.md: documentation indexes

Repository map:
- src/app/: Next.js App Router pages and UI
- src/app/api/: HTTP Route Handlers
- src/domain/assessment/: strict Zod schemas, deterministic calculation and result DTOs
- src/server/: Prisma, anonymous authentication and API helpers
- prisma/schema.prisma: authoritative database schema
- prisma/migrations/: versioned production migrations
- src/generated/prisma/: generated code; never hand-edit
- tests/unit/: domain and route tests
- tests/e2e/: mobile browser and real-PostgreSQL API tests
- scripts/: deploy, backup and demo replay tools
- deploy/nginx/: reverse proxy configuration

Seven step keys:
- gender
- goal
- age
- height
- weight
- target-weight
- activity

Critical invariants:
- Raw anonymous token only in an HttpOnly Cookie; database stores SHA-256 hash.
- Verify resource ownership for every Session, result and payment request.
- Preserve request idempotency, payload conflict detection and optimistic version locking.
- Validate the complete Profile again on submit.
- Calculate BMI, calories, target date and projection on the server only.
- Use separate allow-listed locked and full result DTO constructors.
- Keep PaymentEvent creation and Subscription activation transactional.
- /pay and /api/pay must remain behaviorally equivalent.
- Mock payment is not real billing; calculations are not medical advice.
- PostgreSQL must not be publicly exposed.
- Current HTTP/IP demo uses fictional data only.

Quality commands:
- npm run db:validate
- npm run lint
- npm run typecheck
- npm test
- npm run build
- npm run test:e2e
- npm audit --audit-level=high
- git diff --check

AI change rules:
- Read AGENTS.md and the relevant node_modules/next/dist/docs/ guide before changing Next.js behavior.
- Do not edit generated Prisma files; update prisma/schema.prisma and migrations.
- Add regression tests for fixes and run the complete quality gate.
- Update both docs/en/ and docs/zh-CN/ when contracts or behavior change.
- Never commit .env files, credentials, server passwords, production tokens or real health data.
